news.title: 'Godspeed my friend': Inside the final hours of Spirit Airlines
news.summary: Spirit Airlines ceased operations overnight after a failed government bailout. Spirit Airlines was one of the largest U.S. airlines. The airline was bailed out by the federal government in 2008.

news.title: U.S. crude oil exports surge to record as tankers flock to Gulf Coast during Iran war
news.summary: Oil exports are booming from the U.S. Gulf Coast thanks to the Middle East supply disruption caused by the Iran war. Oil exports from the Gulf Coast are booming thanks to a Middle Eastern supply disruption.

news.title: OPEC+ announces 188,000 barrels-per-day output increase in first meeting without UAE
news.summary: Concerns around production were amplified further last week with news of the UAE's shock departure. The UAE is one of the biggest oil producers in the world. The country is also the biggest producer of crude oil.

news.title: Greg Abel rules out Berkshire break-up, stresses continuity with Buffett's legacy at annual meeting
news.summary: New CEO Greg Abel did most of the talking at his first Berkshire Hathaway shareholders meeting. Warren Buffett was still a major presence at the meeting.

news.title: Healthcare cost surge makes generous parental paid leave benefits a target of workplace cuts
news.summary: As healthcare costs soar and companies search for savings, generous paid parental leave is a benefit being cut. Recent examples from Zoom and Deloitte.

news.title: S&P 500 profits haven’t been this rich in at least 15 years — but there’s more to the story
news.summary: Three Big Tech companies have had an outsize impact on first-quarter profit performance. The companies are Apple, Google and Facebook. The tech companies are expected to report quarterly results on Thursday.

news.title: ‘The numbers don’t lie’: If I had invested my Social Security in the S&P 500 I’d have $4 million. Is the system broken?
news.summary: “I do better than many citizens because I’ve contributed at the highest level,” he says. “I’m not just a politician. I am a citizen.”

news.title: ‘I am her scapegoat’: My mother-in-law squandered all her money. Do we buy her a house so she’s not homeless?
news.summary: “She blames me for her poor relationship with her son and grandchildren,” he says. “She’s very angry with me.”

news.title: ‘We’re all worried the honey pot will run dry’: Does the U.S. government borrow from my Social Security to fund federal programs?
news.summary: There are many suggestions floating around Washington D.C. on how to fix the program’s imminent funding issues. There are also many suggestions on what to do to keep the program going.

news.title: ‘I hope to retire early’: I’m 56 and have 80% in a traditional IRA and 20% in a Roth. Am I in trouble?
news.summary: “We have $3.5 million in savings — $2. 5 million of which are in retirement accounts,” he says. “We’re in a good place.”
